title: David McFarlane: Nexaweb Platform 4.5 Supports Ajax, Java
author: Outlook Series (officeroutlook.com)
abstract: Outlook Series interview-style press coverage (2006-12-18) announcing Nexaweb Platform 4.5, which pairs the Universal Client Framework (UCF) for Ajax/Java with Section 508 accessibility compliance. Peter S. Kastner (Research VP, technology integration at Aberdeen Group) cites Aberdeen research that two-thirds of survey respondents view improving services to IT users as a key business requirement and endorses Nexaweb's composite-application approach for rich cross-platform experiences that preserve legacy IT assets.
importance_rationale: Adds Section 508 accessibility and formal data-services capability milestone to the Nexaweb EW2.0 timeline; captures Aberdeen 2006 survey data on user-services priorities.
relevance_rationale: Nexaweb platform is discontinued; Ajax RIA paradigm supplanted by HTML5 and SPA frameworks. Section 508 accessibility remains a live concern but framework approaches differ.
prescience_rationale: Kastner endorsement implied durable growth for Nexaweb-style enterprise Web 2.0 platforms; these were eclipsed within a few years by HTML5, mobile, and SPAs.
